_comment: Canonical normalization vectors for Proto-Commons Record Format Spec 6.3 / 9.9. Every conforming implementation of the key-normalization pipeline (NFC -> Unicode case fold -> NFC -> whitespace-collapse -> trim) MUST map each `input` to the `expected` sequence of Unicode code points (lowercase hex, no prefix). This file is the cross-implementation determinism gate. Inputs are literal UTF-8 (the compose vector is intentionally NFD: U+0045 U+0301). The `expected` arrays carry the unambiguous code points. This file is mirrored byte-for-byte in commons-tool at internal/indexer/testdata/normalization-vectors.json; keep them in sync.

vectors.name: latin-capital-i-with-dot-above
vectors.note: U+0130. Python lower() expands to i+combining-dot; Go ToLower keeps 1:1 'i'. Fold (CaseFolding.txt F-mapping) gives the expanded form on every runtime; the second NFC pass leaves it as two code points (no precomposed form exists).

vectors.name: sharp-s
vectors.note: U+00DF folds to 'ss'. Deliberate expansion vs lower() (which keeps the sharp-s).

vectors.name: greek-final-sigma
vectors.note: U+03C2 (final sigma) folds to the same key as U+03A3/U+03C3 -- caseless and position-independent.

vectors.name: turkish-dotless-i
vectors.note: U+0131 has no fold mapping and is left unchanged. The pipeline is language-independent: no Turkish dotted/dotless tailoring is applied.

vectors.name: nfc-compose-then-fold
vectors.note: 'E' + combining acute (U+0301) -> NFC composes to U+00C9 -> fold to U+00E9 -> NFC leaves U+00E9. Exercises step 1 (NFC compose) + step 2 (fold) together.

vectors.name: mixed-whitespace-and-sharp-s
vectors.note: Leading/trailing spaces, a tab, and a newline collapse+trim to a single internal space; both sharp-s expand to 'ss'. Result: 'schloss strasse'. Exercises steps 1-5 together.
